EIFS Contractor in Brazos County, TX

Star Stucco of Austin provides certified EIFS installation and repair in Brazos County. From university-adjacent developments in College Station to commercial properties in Bryan, our team delivers Exterior Insulation and Finish Systems designed for the climate and building demands of the Brazos Valley.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

EIFS Expertise for the Brazos Valley

Brazos County is anchored by the twin cities of Bryan and College Station, home to Texas A&M University and a steadily growing population that fuels both residential and commercial construction. The area's post-oak savannah landscape and humid subtropical climate create conditions where buildings need effective moisture management and strong thermal performance. EIFS addresses both requirements with a system that combines continuous insulation to reduce cooling loads with a managed drainage plane that handles the Brazos Valley's considerable rainfall.

The university's influence shapes much of the county's building activity. Student housing complexes, retail centers, restaurants, and mixed-use developments around the campus all benefit from EIFS as a cladding that meets energy codes, installs efficiently on multi-story structures, and provides the architectural variety needed to differentiate one project from the next. Residential neighborhoods throughout College Station and Bryan also use EIFS for its combination of curb appeal and energy savings.

Our team understands the Brazos County market, from the fast-paced commercial development near Texas A&M to the residential communities expanding south and east of Bryan. We bring the same certified EIFS expertise and attention to local conditions that define our work across the greater Central Texas region.

What's Included With Our EIFS Contractor in Brazos County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

EIFS System Evaluation

We assess building substrates, exposure, and the humidity conditions typical of the Brazos Valley to specify an EIFS system optimized for your property.

Insulation Board Installation

Rigid EPS insulation is applied continuously across the wall assembly, reducing heat gain during the long, humid summers that characterize Brazos County.

Base Coat & Mesh Application

A polymer-modified base coat reinforced with fiberglass mesh provides impact resistance and a stable surface for the decorative finish.

Finish Coat & Texturing

Acrylic finishes are applied in colors and textures suited to the project, whether it is a maroon-and-white themed commercial building near campus or a residential home in a Bryan neighborhood.

Sealant & Flashing Details

Every window, door, and wall transition is sealed and flashed to handle the substantial rainfall that the Brazos Valley receives, particularly during tropical weather events.

Final Inspection & Walkthrough

A thorough quality check covers adhesion, drainage, and finish consistency. We walk you through maintenance practices suited to the humid Brazos County environment.

Common EIFS Contractor Needs in Brazos County

Brazos County's active construction market generates a variety of EIFS project types. Here are the services we provide most often to Bryan-College Station area property owners.

EIFS Installation on New Construction

Student housing, retail, restaurant, and residential projects across the Bryan-College Station market use EIFS to meet energy codes and create distinctive exterior designs. We coordinate with general contractors to deliver EIFS on tight construction schedules.

EIFS Repair & Patching

Storm damage, vandalism, and wear from the humid climate can affect EIFS surfaces. We repair damaged areas with matching materials to maintain both the appearance and performance of the wall system.

Moisture Intrusion Remediation

The Brazos Valley's high humidity and heavy rainfall can stress older EIFS without modern drainage. We investigate moisture problems, repair substrates, install proper drainage provisions, and rebuild the EIFS assembly.

Foam Trim & Architectural Details

Custom foam trim — columns, cornices, window surrounds, and banding — adds architectural detail to Brazos County buildings without the weight of masonry, which is particularly useful on multi-story student housing and mixed-use projects.

Commercial EIFS Systems

The commercial corridors of Bryan and College Station rely on EIFS for energy-efficient, visually diverse exteriors. We handle large-scale installations with multi-phase scheduling to accommodate tenant occupancy and campus activity.

EIFS-to-Stucco Conversion

Property owners who want to convert older barrier EIFS to traditional stucco or a modern drainage EIFS turn to us for complete removal, substrate assessment, and professional re-cladding.

Why Choose Us for EIFS Contractor in Brazos County?

  • Factory-certified in Sto Corp, Dryvit, and Senergy/BASF EIFS systems with credentials for manufacturer warranty coverage
  • Experienced with the humid subtropical conditions of the Brazos Valley, where moisture management is a top priority
  • Energy performance expertise that helps College Station and Bryan property owners comply with codes and reduce utility costs
  • Proven track record on multi-story commercial and multi-family projects common in the university market
  • Manufacturer warranties on materials and workmanship backed by our certified applicator status
  • Organized project management, clear communication, and detailed proposals from estimate through completion

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Brazos County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our EIFS Contractor Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Contact Us for an EIFS Consultation

Call (512) 706-9699 to schedule a visit to your Brazos County property. We will evaluate the building, discuss your project needs, and present the EIFS options that fit your goals.

2

System Evaluation & Design

We inspect the substrate, assess moisture and humidity factors, and design an EIFS assembly with the insulation, drainage, and finish specifications that will perform in the Brazos Valley climate.

3

Review Scope & Investment

A detailed written proposal covers materials, labor, timeline, and total cost. We review every element with you so the project scope and investment are clear before work begins.

4

Professional Installation

Our certified team installs the EIFS system according to manufacturer standards, manages the jobsite professionally, and completes the project with a thorough inspection and walkthrough.
